examples.text: بَدَأَت جُو تَمْشِي ذَهَابًا وَإِيَابًا وَيَدَاهَا خَلْفَ ظَهْرِهَا، كَٱلرَّجُلِ.
examples.english: Joe started walking back and forth with her hands behind her back, like a man.

senses.gloss: someone absent from the tribe or just returned
to succeed, to follow, to come after
to be detained, to stay away

etymology.text: From Proto-Semitic *ḫalap-. Cognate with Biblical Hebrew חָלַף (ḥɔlap̄, “to move past”).

of the one whom thou hast lost:
(S, Msb, K:) thus one says to one who has lost by death his father (S, Msb, K) or mother (K) or paternal uncle (S, Msb) or any other who cannot be replaced: (Msb, K:) and
: (K: [in which it is implied that these phrases mean
May God supply to thee well the place of him whom thou hast lost:
but it is implied in the Msb that the two of them there mentioned mean
May God restore to thee good in the place of that which has gone from thee:

and it appears from what here follows that all of these phrases have the latter meaning, whether or not they have the former meaning also:]) to him who has lost property or a child or a thing [of any other kind] of which the replacement may be asked, (S,) or to him of whom that which may be replaced has perished, or died, (K,) one says,
May God restore to thee the like of that which has gone from thee,
may God restore to thee what has gone from thee;
(K in a later part of the art.;) and
is allowable in relation to property and the like; and
is allowable as its aor., though extr., (K,) as it has no faucial letter to occasion the fet-h: (TA:) and one says also,
May God give thee good in the place of that which has gone from thee;
(K;) and if so, its inf. n. is
he became in the place of his father;
(K;) and if so, its inf. n. is
he became in the place of his father, exclusively of every other.

Such a man took as his wife such a woman after another husband [and thus supplied his place].
seized, such a one from behind him;
He came to him from behind him, and smote his neck,
struck off his head, with the sword.

and Az says that this is a more correct explanation than the former one. (TA.) [Hence, app.,] one says also,

body.text: In these people are such as supply the place of those who have gone.
In such a one is a substitute for such a one
He is a substitute for his father.

maA laka tarogiyna walaA taroguwA Alxalifo
What aileth thee that thou utterest a grumbling cry, when the pregnant camels utter not that cry?
